note: Engineering and Technology programmes focus on applied, industry-oriented training with strong laboratory and workshop components.
Cut-off points, fees and admission requirements are indicative and should be verified from official university publications.

courses.course_name: A Four-year Bachelor Of Technology In Renewable Energy Technology

mode: Full-time lectures and laboratory practicals
Field trips to renewable energy installations
Mandatory industrial attachment
entry_requirements: WASSCE/SSSCE with credits (A1-C6/A-D) in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant electives including Physics, Elective Mathematics and one of Chemistry or Applied Electricity/Technical Drawing
Overall aggregate not exceeding 32 (estimated)
HND and other relevant diploma holders may apply for top-up entry
level_note: Programme trains students in the design, installation and maintenance of renewable energy systems.
Graduates support Ghana’s energy transition and rural electrification initiatives.

courses.course_name: A Two-year Diploma In Renewable Energy Technology

mode: Full-time classroom and workshop sessions
Hands-on fieldwork and basic installations
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
At least three passes in relevant electives such as Physics, Elective Mathematics or Applied Electricity
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature applicants with relevant technical experience may be considered
level_note: Programme prepares technicians to support renewable energy deployment at community and SME level.
Graduates can progress to BTech Renewable Energy Technology through top-up routes.

mode: Full-time lectures and lab-based learning
Industry projects and plant visits
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one other science/technical elective
Competitive aggregate not exceeding 30 (estimated)
Relevant HND/technical diploma holders may be considered for advanced standing
level_note: Students learn to design and maintain automated industrial systems.
Programme supports modernisation and automation in manufacturing and process industries.

courses.course_name: Bachelor Of Technology (BTECH) In Agricultural Engineering (4 Years)

mode: Full-time lectures, field practicals and workshops
Industrial attachment with farms and agro-based industries
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one of Chemistry/Agricultural Science/Technical Drawing
Aggregate not exceeding 36 (estimated)
HND Agricultural Engineering or related fields may gain advanced standing
level_note: Programme focuses on mechanisation and sustainable agricultural systems.
Graduates contribute to productivity improvements in Ghana’s agricultural sector.

courses.course_name: Bachelor Of Technology (BTECH) In Mechanical Engineering (4 Years)

mode: Full-time lectures with intensive workshop practice
Industry-based projects and attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one other science/technical elective
Aggregate not exceeding 32 (estimated)
Relevant HND Mechanical Engineering holders may enter at higher levels
level_note: Programme provides a broad foundation in mechanical engineering principles and applications.
Graduates are prepared for roles in manufacturing, energy, automotive and related industries.

mode: Full-time classroom, design studio and field practicals
Site visits and industrial attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one of Chemistry/Technical Drawing/Building Construction
Aggregate not exceeding 30 (estimated)
HND Civil Engineering holders may be admitted to top-up levels
level_note: Students acquire skills for planning, designing and supervising civil infrastructure projects.
Programme emphasises practical field exposure and construction management.

mode: Full-time lecture and laboratory-based delivery
Design projects and industrial attachments
entry_requirements: Standard WASSCE entry with strong science/technical background
Direct entry from secondary school for 4-year track
Alternative routes for technical and vocational graduates subject to evaluation
level_note: This is the standard full-duration civil engineering pathway from WASSCE level.
Curriculum prepares students for professional registration and further postgraduate study.

mode: Weekend/evening modes may be available for working HND holders
Mix of lectures, design studios and project work
entry_requirements: HND in Civil Engineering or closely related field from an accredited institution
Good HND classification (usually Second Class Lower or better)
Relevant work experience is an advantage
Successful performance at interview and transcript evaluation
level_note: Top-up route for HND holders seeking a bachelor-level qualification in civil engineering.
Focuses on higher-level design, project management and professional skills.

mode: Full-time classroom teaching and field practicals
Short industrial attachments with construction firms
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes in three technical/science electives such as Building Construction, Physics or Technical Drawing
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature candidates with construction experience may be considered
level_note: Programme prepares middle-level civil engineering technicians.
Graduates may progress to HND or BTech Civil Engineering programmes.

mode: Full-time lectures and workshop practicals
Short industrial attachments in mechanical workshops and plants
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes in Physics and at least two other technical/science electives
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Technical/Vocational certificate holders may be admitted after assessment
level_note: Diploma provides foundational mechanical engineering skills for technician roles.
Graduates can progress to HND or BTech Mechanical Engineering.

mode: Full-time lectures, tutorials and lab sessions
Industrial attachments in power, telecoms and manufacturing sectors
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one other science/technical elective
Aggregate not exceeding 30 (estimated)
HND Electrical/Electronic Engineering holders may enter top-up track
level_note: BTech route emphasises applied electrical and electronic engineering skills.
Graduates can work in power utilities, manufacturing, telecom and automation industries.

mode: Full-time lectures and workshop sessions
Industrial attachment with electrical contractors and firms
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes/credits in Physics, Elective Mathematics and one other technical/science subject
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature applicants with electrical trade certificates may be considered
level_note: HND prepares middle-level electrical and electronics technicians.
HND graduates may progress to BTech Electrical and Electronics Engineering.

mode: Full-time classroom and workshop-based learning
Short practical attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes in Physics and other technical/science electives
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
level_note: Diploma equips students with basic electrical and electronic skills for entry-level technician roles.
Graduates can advance to HND or BTech programmes after meeting progression requirements.

mode: Weekend/evening or flexible modes for HND holders
Coursework, labs and design projects
entry_requirements: HND in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or related field from accredited institution
Good HND classification, typically Second Class Lower or better
Relevant industry experience is an advantage
Successful performance at interview and transcript assessment
level_note: Top-up route for HND holders to earn a BTech in Electrical and Electronics Engineering.
Emphasises advanced design, analysis and management skills.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma (HND) In Civil Engineering

mode: Full-time with site and laboratory practicals
Mandatory industrial attachment
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes/credits in relevant electives such as Building Construction, Physics or Technical Drawing
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature candidates with construction experience may be considered
level_note: HND Civil Engineering forms a solid base for site supervisory and technician roles.
Graduates can progress to BTech Civil Engineering through top-up.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma (HND) In Mechanical Engineering

mode: Full-time lectures and workshop practice
Industrial attachment with factories and plants
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Passes/credits in Physics and other technical/science electives
Aggregate not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Technical Institute graduates may be considered
level_note: Programme prepares technicians for plant, maintenance and manufacturing roles.
HND holders may progress to BTech Mechanical Engineering.

courses.course_name: Master Of Technology (MTECH) In Civil Engineering

electives: Finite Element Methods in Civil Engineering
mode: Coursework plus thesis or major project
Evening/weekend options may be available for professionals
entry_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or BTech in Civil Engineering or related discipline with at least Second Class Lower
Relevant industry experience is an advantage
Applicants may be required to attend an interview and submit a research proposal
Holders of strong HND plus significant experience may be considered under special admission
level_note: Programme deepens technical and research capacity in key civil engineering areas.
Graduates are prepared for leadership roles in infrastructure planning, design and management.

mode: Flexible schedules for working HND graduates
Project-based learning and design work
entry_requirements: HND in Mechanical Engineering or closely related area from recognised institution
Good HND classification, normally Second Class Lower or better
Relevant work experience is an advantage
Possible interview and academic transcript review
level_note: Top-up prepares HND holders for professional mechanical engineering positions.
Emphasis on advanced analysis, design and management of mechanical systems.

note: Programmes focus on information management, computing and digital literacy for modern organisations.
Students are trained using up-to-date tools and industry-relevant case studies.

mode: Full-time lectures and practical sessions
Internships in libraries, archives, and information centres
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science/Social Studies
Credits in three relevant electives such as ICT, Economics, Geography or Literature in English
Aggregate not exceeding 36 (estimated)
Diploma/Professional certificate holders in information-related areas may be considered for top-up
level_note: Programme blends information management, technology and organisational skills.
Graduates support information services in public and private sector organisations.

note: Programmes under this faculty develop creative and technical skills for the fashion and creative industries.
Students engage in studio work, practical projects and industry attachments.

salary: Ghana: GHS 30,000 - 80,000 annually (estimated, highly variable for entrepreneurs)
mode: Weekend classes tailored for working students
Studio-based practical sessions and projects
entry_requirements: Relevant HND or Diploma in Fashion/Textile or related area for BTech weekend track
Evidence of portfolio or practical experience is an advantage
Interview and portfolio review may be required
level_note: Weekend BTech is designed for diploma/HND holders and industry practitioners upgrading their qualifications.
Focuses on advanced design, production and business aspects of fashion.

mode: Weekend programme for working learners
Practical-focused with studio and workshop sessions
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes in English Language and other core subjects
Evidence of interest/experience in fashion (e.g. apprenticeship) is preferred
Mature candidates and vocational graduates may be considered
level_note: Weekend HND builds foundational fashion and textile skills.
Graduates may progress to BTech or start small fashion enterprises.

mode: Full-time lectures and studio work
Practical sewing and garment construction exercises
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language and other core subjects
Interest or background in fashion, textiles or visual arts is desirable
Mature and vocational applicants may be considered
level_note: Diploma introduces core practical skills in fashion and textile design.
Graduates may progress to HND/BTech or start small-scale fashion businesses.

levels.duration: 3 years (top-up from diploma/HND) or 4 years direct
mode: Full-time studio and classroom instruction
Industry attachments with fashion houses and textile firms
entry_requirements: Relevant HND/Diploma in Fashion/Textile or strong WASSCE background with portfolio
Interview and portfolio review may be required
Mature candidates with substantial industry experience may be considered
level_note: BTech builds advanced creative, technical and entrepreneurial competencies in fashion and textiles.
Graduates can lead fashion labels, manage production units or pursue further study.

mode: Full-time day programme
Studio-based classes and fashion shows/exhibitions
entry_requirements: WASSCE credits/passes in English Language and other core subjects
Visual Arts or Home Economics background is an advantage
Mature applicants with fashion/apprenticeship background may apply
level_note: HND provides strong practical skills and prepares students for BTech top-up or self-employment.
Students build portfolios through projects and fashion events.

mode: Full-time or weekend top-up for HND holders
Studio and industry-based projects
entry_requirements: HND in Fashion Design, Textiles or related area from recognised institution
Good HND classification (normally Second Class Lower or better)
Portfolio and interview may be required
level_note: Top-up consolidates design, production and business knowledge for supervisory and creative leadership roles.
Graduates may run their own brands or work in established fashion houses.

mode: Weekend or flexible modes for working HND graduates (where available)
Project-oriented with strong emphasis on portfolio and brand development
entry_requirements: HND in Fashion Design and Textile Studies or a closely related qualification
Good HND classification and evidence of practical competence
Submission of portfolio and possible interview
level_note: Top-up pathway focused on advanced design and fashion entrepreneurship.
Graduates can lead creative teams or establish competitive fashion brands.

mode: Studio-based classes and computer lab sessions
Design projects and portfolio development
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language and other core subjects
Interest or background in visual arts and design is preferred
Submission of simple creative work or aptitude test may be required
level_note: Diploma introduces core graphic and multimedia design skills.
Graduates can work as junior designers or progress to BTech in Industrial Arts and Design.

courses.course_name: Industrial Arts And Design (Graphic Design And Multimedia Studies Option)

cutt_off: Competitive WASSCE aggregate or good Diploma/HND in design
mode: Full-time studio and lab-based learning
Industry-based projects and internships
entry_requirements: Diploma/HND in Graphic Design, Multimedia or related area for top-up, or strong WASSCE with visual arts background
Portfolio submission and possible interview
Mature applicants with substantial design experience may be considered
level_note: Programme builds advanced visual communication and multimedia competencies.
Graduates can work in advertising, media, branding agencies and digital content studios.

note: Tourism and hospitality programmes combine practical service skills with management and business training.
Students undertake internships in hotels, resorts, and tourism organisations.

courses.course_name: Diploma In Tourism And Hospitality Management

mode: Full-time lectures and practical sessions
Industrial attachment with hotels and tourism firms
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language and other core subjects
Passes in any three electives (Home Economics, Business or General Arts advantageous)
Mature applicants with work experience in hospitality may be considered
level_note: Diploma prepares students for entry-level roles in hospitality and tourism operations.
Graduates may progress to HND or BTech Tourism Management.

courses.course_name: Four-year Bachelor Of Technology In Tourism Management

mode: Full-time classroom and field-based activities
Internships with hotels, resorts and tourism agencies
entry_requirements: WASSCE credits in English Language, Core Mathematics and Social Studies/Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant electives (Business, General Arts, Home Economics or related)
Aggregate not exceeding 36 (estimated)
Diploma/HND in Tourism/Hospitality may gain advanced placement
level_note: Programme focuses on management and development aspects of tourism and hospitality.
Graduates can work in both private tourism enterprises and public tourism agencies.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma In Tourism Management

mode: Full-time lectures and practical exercises
Industrial attachment in hospitality and tourism establishments
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes/credits in English Language and other core subjects
Passes in relevant electives such as Business, Home Economics or General Arts
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature and industry-experienced applicants may be considered
level_note: HND provides solid managerial and operational knowledge for tourism and hospitality.
Graduates may progress to BTech Tourism Management through top-up.

courses.course_name: Two Year Top-up Bachelor Of Technology In Tourism Management

mode: May be offered in weekend or flexible modes
Case studies and project-based learning
entry_requirements: HND in Tourism Management, Hospitality or related discipline
Good HND classification (Second Class Lower or better)
Relevant work experience is an advantage
Interview may be required
level_note: Top-up route for HND graduates to acquire advanced tourism management skills.
Graduates can take up supervisory and managerial roles in tourism and hospitality.

note: Programmes in this faculty prepare students for roles in libraries, archives and heritage institutions.
Emphasis is placed on information organisation, user services and preservation.

mode: Full-time lectures and practical sessions in libraries
Fieldwork and attachments in school and public libraries
entry_requirements: WASSCE passes in English Language and other core subjects
Passes in any three electives (General Arts, Business or Science background acceptable)
Mature applicants working in libraries may be considered with lower aggregates
level_note: Diploma prepares support staff for different types of libraries and information centres.
Graduates may progress to bachelor’s degrees in library/information science.
